#  Custom Projects Review Guidance Documents
[Custom Projects Review Home Page](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/energy-efficiency/custom-projects-review)   
Custom Projects Review Guidance Page  
[Custom Projects Stakeholder Engagement Group](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/demand-side-management/energy-efficiency/custom-projects-review-stakeholder-engagement-page)
The staff from the four investor-owned utilities (IOUs) and CPUC staff collaboratively developed guidance documents for the Custom Projects Review Process. These guidance documents provide details on the Commission’s policies and procedures to be utilized in the development of Custom Projects/Measures. 
Projects developed in the custom process will likely follow a path of project development, pre-installation review, installation and commissioning, and post installation review. These steps have key aspects that must be fulfilled in order to qualify as a Custom Project. The guidance documents on this webpage will assist project developers in submitting complete and concise documentation to support the energy efficiency savings estimates submitted to the IOU. 
##  Project Development 
### Project Development
[Statewide Custom Project Guidance Document, version 1.4](https://file.ac/OEr-2p-bk3A/ "Statewide Custom Project Guidance Document, version 1.4")
The purpose of this Custom Project Guidance document is to provide an overview of the policies, guidelines, rules, processes, requirements and definitions used in Custom Project Reviews documentation and quality assurance. This document provides information on the requirements for submitting a complete custom project application package:
  * Provides key definitions, eligibility, and program influence for Custom Projects
  * Requirements for project packages a various stages
  * Measurement and Verification plan requirements
  * [Old versions of the guidance document](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/~//-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/custom-projects-review-guidance-documents/statewide-custom-project-guidance-documents.zip)


**Project Feasibility Study (PFS)**
This document is a template for Program Administrators (PAs) and implementation to complete when submitting a Custom or SEM project to the CPUC.
[Statewide Project Feasibility Study 2.1](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/custom-projects-review-guidance-documents/statewide-project-feasibility-study-template-21202505.xlsx)
[Old PFS version: do not use Project Feasibility Study Template, version 1.1](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/custom-projects-review-guidance-documents/statewide-project-feasibility-study-v11-sept-2023.docx)
Some customers or project types may be required to submit one or more affidavits along with the completed Project Feasibility Study (PFS). Sample affidavits linked below include:
  * [Small business customer designation](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/custom-projects-review-guidance-documents/affidavit-for-small-business-customers.docx)
  * [Hard-to-reach customer designation](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/custom-projects-review-guidance-documents/affidavit-for-hard-to-reach-customers.docx)
  * [Existing equipment viability (for Accelerated Replacement measures)](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/custom-projects-review-guidance-documents/affidavit-for-existing-equipment-viability.docx)


Please note: The affidavits provided via the links above are intended to serve as example language. Programs Administrators (PAs) may have their own versions of these affidavits. If so, please continue using those versions.
[Map and Timeline of Program Administrator's (PA) Pre-Installation Process](https://file.ac/TBUupreNpH0/)
This is an informational guide for stakeholders on the timeline and steps taken during the PA's pre-installation Custom Project Review process.
  * Timeline with responsibilities by stakeholders


[Early Opinion Form](https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/-/media/cpuc-website/divisions/energy-division/documents/energy-efficiency/custom-projects-review-guidance-documents/cpuc-early-opinion-form.docx)
This document is a form for Program Administrators (PAs) to complete when requesting an Early Opinion (EO) from the CPUC. EOs are used for custom projects in the development stage that require CPUC guidance on specific issues or areas of uncertainty. Topics can vary widely, including measure eligibility or the implementation of specific CPUC guidance or policy.
The PA submits an EO request form, which includes background and supporting information on the topic, as well as their own opinion on the specific issue. The form is intended to be updated and exchanged between the PA and the CPUC until a resolution is reached. The CPUC will then summarize and provide guidance on the issue and, if applicable, share the information more broadly.
##  Pre-Installation Review 
### Pre-Installation Review
[Project Selection and Response Timing Proposal](https://file.ac/xaZDtgORC10/)
This document details the CPUC staff a day 30-day timeline for reviewing Custom Projects as mandated in [SB 1131](http://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/billNavClient.xhtml?bill_id=201720180SB1131). It also covers the communication timing between the CPUC and the utility during the 30-day review.
  * Tracking of response due dates
  * SB 1131 requirements
  * CPUC staff recommendation timeline examples


[Recommended Communication Practices for Custom Measure Review of Large Projects](https://file.ac/LieyfIAZ8oc/)
The primary goal of this proposed Communications Practice for Large EE Projects (Expected incentive greater than $100,000) is to facilitate the Custom Measure Review Process and promote understanding of project parameters between Implementers, PA/Reviewers and Commission Staff ("CS")/Consultants to identify key issues early in the review cycle. This will likely reduce the back and forth between Implementers and Reviewers, thereby reducing total review time. These communications are intended to be informal and not result in additional time delays in the formal PA or CPUC staff project review processes. The document was created by a working group that included utilities, implementers, and CPUC staff.
  * Project specific communication
  * Overall custom measurement review communication


[Custom Project Disposition Database](http://www.caenergyguidance.com/ "Custom Project Disposition Database")
Once CPUC staff completes the review of Custom Projects, they will issue to the Program Administrator a review findings disposition document summarizing any issues or comments related to the project eligibility, baseline, energy efficiency savings calculations, or program influence documentation. This searchable public database contains Program Administrator redacted versions of the CPUC staff dispositions, as well as ISP studies, guidance documents, and other Custom Projects related documents.
  * Database of dispositions and other documents


##  Installation and Commissioning 
### Installation and Commissioning
The CPUC does not have any requirements on installation and commissioning. Please refer to your Program Administrator (PA) for any questions on this stage.
##  Post Installation Review 
### Post Installation Review
[Post Installation Summary Report, v1.0](https://file.ac/2dwQX9bc2bc/)
This document is a template for implementers/project developers to use and fill out as the post-installation report that is to be submitted to the Program Administrator (PA). The Installation Summary Report is a complete Custom Project summary report which summarizes all project documentation into a single source for a reviewer to look at and understand how the energy savings were validated and present the savings to the PA in an easily readable format.
  * Template report for Post-Installation projects
  * Guidelines for Custom Project requirements for Post Installation
  * Summarized report for savings validation

### Sheet: SEM
|   Unnamed: 0 | Back to READ ME                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |   Unnamed: 2 |   Unnamed: 3 | 'EEM 1' tab   |   Unnamed: 5 | Unnamed: 6                                                        |
|-------------:|:---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------:|-------------:|:--------------|-------------:|:------------------------------------------------------------------|
|          nan | This worksheet is not applicable to your project.  Please continue to the next tab (EEM1).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | California SEM Measure and Verification (M&V) Guide, Version 3.02 |
|          nan | nan                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | Initial Project Submittal, Selection, and Disposition:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | • SEM projects seeking SEM-program based incentives should be included in the bi-monthly submission list at the end of the first reporting period.                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | • Projects not selected for CPR at time of initial listing do not need to submit a PFS, but will need to be listed again after the first reporting period of each new two-year cycle.                                                                                                                                                                                |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | • Projects selected for CPR:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | o Dispositions for these projects are advisory-only.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | o These projects need to submit a completed PFS within two weeks of CPR project selection.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | o These projects do not need to be relisted on the bi-monthly submission list in any subsequent two-year cycle.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | o For projects that do not have viable models or calculations indicating savings within the first year, CPUC may request additional documentation beyond the first year.                                                                                                                                                                                             |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | nan                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | nan                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | The project documentation package (submitted along with the PFS) must include:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | nan                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | Documentation type                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |          nan |          nan | File name(s)  |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | Year 1 Completion Report (Reporting Performance Period Report - RPPR):                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|              | Documentation that meets the requirements of Section 14.1.2 through Section 14.1.7 of the M&V Guide shall be provided.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |              |              |               |              |                                                                   |
|          nan | Opportunity Register:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|              | Includes energy performance and Energy Management System (EnMS) improvement opportunities identified and completed through SEM engagement.                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |              |              |               |              |                                                                   |
|          nan | ((If a bottom-up approach is being sought))                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|              | Notification of Bottom-up Method of Determining Energy Savings (NOBU):                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |              |              |               |              |                                                                   |
|              | The NOBU should contain:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |              |              |               |              |                                                                   |
|          nan | • Statement describing efforts taken to-date to create energy consumption adjustment models.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | • Justification for not further pursuing energy consumption adjustment models and switching to the bottom-up approach.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | • Discussion of what efforts can and will be taken to enable the development of energy consumption adjustment models in subsequent Reporting Periods.                                                                                                                                                                                                                |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | Model or Savings Calculation:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|              | If claiming savings for either top-down or bottom-up savings calculations. File should include granular production and meter data (not just cumulative data).                                                                                                                                                                                                        |              |              |               |              |                                                                   |
|          nan | Scoping Summary:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|              | This document describes the conditions that existed at each site prior to SEM Engagement, focused primarily on energy efficiency history and plans.                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |              |              |               |              |                                                                   |
|          nan | nan                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | As available, additional documentation may include:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | nan                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | Documentation type                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |          nan |          nan | File name(s)  |          nan | nan                                                               |
|          nan | Treasure Hunt Summary:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|              | Provides details on the outcomes of the treasure hunt.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |              |              |               |              |                                                                   |
|          nan | Energy Data Collection Plan:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|              | Provides information describing when and how data should be collected from identified data sources. It will address the collection of energy consumption and relevant variable data.                                                                                                                                                                                 |              |              |               |              |                                                                   |
|          nan | Energy Savings Report:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|              | If applicable and separate from RPPR. Includes a more detailed description of the statistical validity of the model.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |              |              |               |              |                                                                   |
|          nan | Technical Review:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|              | If applicable. Sometimes the claimed savings overrides the savings from the model or bottom-up calculations. This document provides support for the changes to modeled or calculated savings.                                                                                                                                                                        |              |              |               |              |                                                                   |
|          nan | Greenhouse Gas and IDSM Summary:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |          nan |          nan | nan           |          nan | nan                                                               |
|              | If available, from workshop summaries. Documentation indicating that the customer has conveyed their interest, or not, regarding the reduction of greenhouse gases and pursuing integrated demand-side management (IDSM) measures and is informed how the SEM M&V process can help their reporting needs if they choose to pursue either of these kinds of measures. |              |              |               |              |                                                                   |

| nan                                                                                                                              | Is this a fuel substitution measure?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | nan                                                                   | nan          | nan          | nan                   | nan                                                                       | nan                        | nan                                                                | nan                | Use the RACC-FSC_v3.0 to calculate source energy savings and CO2 offsets for this measure.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | RACC-FSC_v3.0 / Technical Guidance Document(s)                     | nan                                                                 |
|                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |                                                                    |                                                                     |
|                                                                                                                                  | Per the RACC-FSC Technical Guidance Document:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    | For custom (i.e., CIAC) measures, please see:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |                                                                    |                                                                     |
|                                                                                                                                  | "Fuel substitution measures, in the context of energy efficiency programs, involve energy efficiency projects where all or a portion of the existing energy use is converted from one fuel to another (i.e., natural gas to electricity or vice versa). Only equipment powered by electricity and/or natural gas fuels and provided by a CPUC-regulated investor-owned utility or a municipal utility are eligible to participate under fuel substitution measures. Measures involving non-utility (unregulated) fuels, such as propane or fuel oil, are termed as fuel switching measures. " |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    |  • columns DO:DT of the '2 RACC' worksheet for avoided refirergant costs/benefits                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |                                                                    |                                                                     |
|                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    |  • columns BT:CT of the '3 FSC' worksheet for the energy savings and avoided emissions                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                    |                                                                     |
| nan                                                                                                                              | Does this measure involve adding or replacing equipment that uses refrigerant and involve a change to the refrigerant type / charge?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | nan                                                                   | nan          | nan          | nan                   | nan                                                                       | nan                        | nan                                                                | nan                | Use the RACC-FSC_v3.0 to calculate refrigerant leakage emissions and avoided costs of net refrigerant leakage emissions for this measure.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | nan                                                                | nan                                                                 |
| nan                                                                                                                              | If applicable, what type of refrigerant does the existing equipment use?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | nan                                                                   | nan          | nan          | nan                   | nan                                                                       | nan                        | nan                                                                | nan                | Select the type(s) of refrigerant(s) from the drop town menu for both the existing and proposed equipment.  Select 'Not applicable' for the existing refrigerant type if this measure only involves adding equipment that uses refrigerant.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | nan                                                                | nan                                                                 |
| nan                                                                                                                              | What type of refrigerant will the proposed equipment use?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | nan                                                                   | nan          | nan          | nan                   | nan                                                                       | nan                        | nan                                                                | nan                | nan                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | nan                                                                | nan                                                                 |
| * Only customers with Non-IOU Energy Sources                                                                                     | Non-IOU Energy Source                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | nan                                                                   | nan          | nan          | nan                   | nan                                                                       | nan                        | nan                                                                | nan                | Please include the effects of your onsite generation in your calculations. Please include whether monthly or hourly analysis is required for each existing non-IOU energy source on-site. A reference to the calculation document should be included here in column G.                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Non-IOU Energy Eligibility Guidance Document                       | nan                                                                 |

|                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |                                                                    |                                                                     |
|                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    |   • Supporting file number(s) for calculation methodology in Row 43                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |                                                                    |                                                                     |
|                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    |   • Comprehensive details on inputs (i.e., assumptions, equations, metered data, spec sheet, energy saving principles)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                    |                                                                     |
|                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    |   • References to any study and data source and notes on all assumptions                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |                                                                    |                                                                     |
|                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    |   • Complete calibration of energy simulation models (if applicable)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |                                                                    |                                                                     |
|                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |                                                                    |                                                                     |
|                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    | For custom spreadsheet analyses, the calculation methodology must be clearly presented and summarized in a single place (e.g., dedicated tab in a workbook / spreadsheet).  It is not realistic to expect that a technical reviewer will search cell by cell, tab by tab through a spreadsheet to ascertain how savings impacts are calculated.                                                                                                                                      |                                                                    |                                                                     |
|                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |                                                                    |                                                                     |
|                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    | Initial estimates of a custom measure’s savings impacts are often performed using “placeholder” calculations which are estimates based on accepted engineering principals and assumptions, the basis of which must be clearly documented and reasonable. For many custom projects, the final savings claims estimates are based on pre-installation and post-installation measurements with a savings calculation methodology that is different than the “placeholder” calculations. |                                                                    |                                                                     |

| nan                                                                                                                              | Is this a fuel substitution measure?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | nan                                                                   | nan          | nan          | nan                   | nan                                                                       | nan                        | nan                                                                | nan                | Use the RACC-FSC_v3.0 to calculate source energy savings and CO2 offsets for this measure.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | RACC-FSC_v3.0 / Technical Guidance Document(s)                     | nan                                                                 |
|                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |                                                                    |                                                                     |
|                                                                                                                                  | Per the RACC-FSC Technical Guidance Document:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    | For custom (i.e., CIAC) measures, please see:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |                                                                    |                                                                     |
|                                                                                                                                  | "Fuel substitution measures, in the context of energy efficiency programs, involve energy efficiency projects where all or a portion of the existing energy use is converted from one fuel to another (i.e., natural gas to electricity or vice versa). Only equipment powered by electricity and/or natural gas fuels and provided by a CPUC-regulated investor-owned utility or a municipal utility are eligible to participate under fuel substitution measures. Measures involving non-utility (unregulated) fuels, such as propane or fuel oil, are termed as fuel switching measures. " |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    |  • columns DO:DT of the '2 RACC' worksheet for avoided refirergant costs/benefits                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |                                                                    |                                                                     |
|                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                       |              |              |                       |                                                                           |                            |                                                                    |                    |  • columns BT:CT of the '3 FSC' worksheet for the energy savings and avoided emissions                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                    |                                                                     |
| nan                                                                                                                              | Does this measure involve adding or replacing equipment that uses refrigerant and involve a change to the refrigerant type / charge?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | nan                                                                   | nan          | nan          | nan                   | nan                                                                       | nan                        | nan                                                                | nan                | Use the RACC-FSC_v3.0 to calculate refrigerant leakage emissions and avoided costs of net refrigerant leakage emissions for this measure.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | nan                                                                | nan                                                                 |
| nan                                                                                                                              | If applicable, what type of refrigerant does the existing equipment use?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | nan                                                                   | nan          | nan          | nan                   | nan                                                                       | nan                        | nan                                                                | nan                | Select the type(s) of refrigerant(s) from the drop town menu for both the existing and proposed equipment.  Select 'Not applicable' for the existing refrigerant type if this measure only involves adding equipment that uses refrigerant.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | nan                                                                | nan                                                                 |
